topic.title: Transportation Planning Strategies (Manual, Interactive & Automatic Planning)

learningObjectives.title: Understand planning strategies
learningObjectives.description: Differentiate manual, interactive, automatic and background planning approaches.

learningObjectives.title: Select appropriate strategy
learningObjectives.description: Align strategy selection with shipment complexity, volume and control requirements.

learningObjectives.title: Understand planner role
learningObjectives.description: Clarify how planner intervention changes across strategy types.

sections.content: SAP TM supports multiple transportation planning strategies to accommodate different business scenarios. Organizations may choose manual planning, interactive planning or fully automatic planning depending on shipment complexity, business rules and operational requirements.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: The planning strategy determines who makes planning decisions-the transportation planner, the optimization engine or both together.

An e-commerce company plans over 20,000 deliveries every day. SAP TM automatically creates Freight Orders overnight using background planning. Transportation planners review only exception cases in the morning.

sections.title: Best Practice
sections.content: Use automatic planning for repetitive daily shipments and manual planning only for exceptional or high-priority transportation requests.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A global retail company schedules automatic transportation planning every night at 1:00 AM. More than 95% of shipments are planned automatically, while planners manually handle only exception Freight Units.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Interactive Planning is the most commonly implemented strategy because it combines optimizer efficiency with planner expertise.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Automatic planning does not eliminate planners. Transportation planners still manage exceptions, urgent shipments and optimization validation.

items: SAP TM supports Manual, Interactive and Automatic Planning.
Interactive Planning is the most common implementation.
Automatic Planning is ideal for large shipment volumes.
Manual Planning is used for exceptional transportation scenarios.
